figures.caption: Table 1: Main results on GEdit-Bench [27] (EN split). The best Overall score is in bold.
Table 2: Main results on KRIS-Bench [46]. Closed-source proprietary models are marked with †. The best Overall score is in bold.

Figure 1: Motivation for inline critic. Top: On a frozen model, we add lightweight heads at intermediate blocks (L6–L54) to probe the generation outputs, with the original final output at L60. Middle: Each head runs its own multi-step denoising to generate an image. Bottom: Based on the final output, we compute a MSE map against the ground truth in each block. After 8x pooling and rank-normalization, we measure the Spearman correlation between each intermediate map and the final map. Results: Although generation capability only emerges in the last few blocks, the spatial error pattern is set far earlier. Even at L6, agreement with the final-layer map already reaches ρ = 0.83, and grows monotonically with depth. We therefore ask whether this prior can be exploited within the forward pass to let the model recognize and correct its weak regions in time.
Table 3: Main results on RISEBench [52] (%). Closed-source proprietary models are marked with †. The best Overall score among open-source editors is in bold.
Figure 5: Qualitative results. We visualize the probe-layer error maps across backbone layers (L6→L54), together with the input image (left) and the final edited result (right).

Figure 2: Three-stage training of Inline Critic. Left: Stage 1 trains probe heads at several intermediate layers to predict the generation target from frozen hidden states. Middle: Stage 2 adds a critic token that is trained, at every probed block, to predict the error of Stage-1’s probes; an attention mask hides it from other tokens so the model and the probes are unaffected. Right: Stage 3 removes the mask and trains the critic jointly with the generation loss to steer generation.
Figure 3: Heterogeneity results on ImgEdit. Bins are formed by baseline performance (left) and edit type (right). Our method’s improvement concentrates on the hard cases.
Figure 4: Top: per-sample histogram of ρ between the different model-side spatial map and the critic’s prediction. Bottom: one sample showing the spatial map (left) and critic’s prediction (right).
